Phase changes
heat = energy
increase energy -> decrease bond stability

Properties of Water
solid is less dense than liquid
ice floats - insulation
H-bonds stable
lattice forms
H-bonds unstable
molecules move, pack together

Properties of Water
solid is less dense than liquid
ice floats – insulation
2) water is cohesive – surface tension

Properties of Water
solid is less dense than liquid
2) water is cohesive – surface tension
movement of water/sap in plants

Properties of Water
solid is less dense than liquid
2) water is cohesive
3) water has a high specific heat/high heat capacity
1 calorie = energy to raise 1 g water 1° C
evaporative cooling
oceans, large lakes – stabilize climate

Properties of Water
solid is less dense than liquid
2) water is cohesive
3) water has a high specific heat/high heat capacity
4) dissociation: acid-base properties

H 2 O    OH - +     H +
in pure water   [H + ] = 10 -7 M
log (10 -7 ) = -7
- log (10 -7 ) = 7
pH = 7.0 defined as “neutral”
pH of pure water
pH < 7.0    acidic				  acids release H + , increase [H + ]
pH > 7.0   basic/alkaline		  bases pick up H + , decrease [H + ]
